sections.paragraph:
φίλανδρος, ον (Aeschyl. et al., and esp. freq. in Plut. w. cognates) having affection/love for a husband (so Phalaris, Ep. 132 φ. καὶ σώφρων; Ep. 58 of Apollonius of Tyana [Philostrat. I 361, 30]; Plut., Mor. 142a φίλανδροι καὶ σώφρονες γυναῖκες; SIG 783, 39 [I b.c.]; SEG XLII, 1277, 4; 1211 A, 2 [superl.]; ins fr. Perg. in Dssm., LO 268 [LAE 314]; PCairMasp 310, 18; PLond V, 1711, 40; Philo, Exs. 139; Cat. Cod. Astr. XII 179, 9.—φιλανδρία: SEG XLII 1214, 11f; Jos., Ant. 18, 159.—Dibelius, Hdb. ad loc.) Tit 2:4; AcPl BMM verso 28 (text uncertain: φιλά]νδρω̣ν).—DELG s.v. ἀνήρ. New Docs 3, 42f (ins, reff.). M-M.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

esp. esp. = especially

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

superl. superl. = superlative

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

AcPl BMM AcPl BMM = Acts of Paul, as reconstructed in part from various papyri, Berlin and Michigan—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1055
